evals.prompt: Use the doca-urom skill to help me. I'm working on: doing hands-on DOCA UROM library work from the host side — wiring doca-urom under an HPC / UCX / MPI stack to OFFLOAD remote memory operations (puts, gets, atomics, collectives) onto a BlueField DPU, creating a UROM Service context (doca_urom_service_*) and Worker contexts (doca_urom_worker_*) that run plugins on the DPU, discovering plugins via doca_urom_service_get_plugins_list, progressing completions, or debugging DOCA_ERROR_* from a doca_urom_* call.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the doca-urom skill and answers from its documented workflow, quoting only real symbols/commands from the bundle (no invented tokens).
assertions: The doca-urom skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
Every symbol, flag, or command named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "DOCA UROM". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-urom issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-urom skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "MPI all-reduce burning host CPU". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-urom issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-urom skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I actually need help with UROM Service deployment on the DPU side. Which skill should handle this, and why is it not doca-urom?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-urom; it explains that UROM Service deployment on the DPU side is out of doca-urom's scope and routes to the appropriate skill instead.
assertions: doca-urom is not selected as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s) instead.
